>> How do you actually go about teaching

>> So, I do that in three ways. First
thing, I have just a list of the most
common connected speech phrases,
sentences. For example, like going to
would switch to going to, have to would
switch to have to. And so in my YouTube
videos and in my course, I just go
through the most common connected speech
things and I give you several examples.
Then I show actually drama clips. You
can see it here in the Spider-Man movie
or whatever. The next thing is analyzing
actual clips from dramas and series.
Just going through line by line. This is
what it means. Okay, let's listen again.
Notice that they're actually saying,
"Did you have lunch yet?" Right? Not did
you. Did you? And the last thing is
vocabulary. Now, vocabulary isn't
something we can ignore completely. We
have to still continue to upgrade our
vocabulary because there's a difference
between textbook vocabulary and real
spoken vocabulary. For example, when you
say bye to someone, goodbye. Native
speakers actually do say goodbye. But we
also say, "Have a good one. Take care.
Talk soon. catch you later. And these
are all natural, but you don't learn
them all in a textbook. Right.
>> Exactly.
